notes: Both round-1 findings resolved cleanly in commit 31bd1d0. (1) Cross-wire guard: each of llmSupportImage/Audio/Thinking_true_parsedCorrectly now asserts assertFalse on the other two flags — a routing bug like `llmSupportImage = llmSupportAudio` in toModel() would now fail the positive test (target flag becomes false) AND the cross-wire assertFalse (contaminated flag becomes true). (2) Edge cases: added defaultConfig_systemPromptDefault_explicitNull_defaultsEmpty (literal JSON null) and defaultConfig_missingEntirely_systemPromptDefaultEmpty (outer object absent). Both route through preProcess() to configValues[SYSTEM_PROMPT_DEFAULT.label] and assert "" — fencing the `.orEmpty()` on ModelAllowlist.kt:60 for the two distinct Gson codepaths (explicit null literal vs missing-field short-circuit). Litmus test: removing `.orEmpty()` would produce null in the configValues Map<String,String> (NPE / null-string) and break both new tests. No regressions: all 7 pre-existing tests (loadFromFixture*, fixtureMatchesProductionAsset, parse_rejects*) untouched in the diff; only net-additive changes. No new smells: no shared state, no mocks, no ordering dependency, naming follows subject_condition_expectation convention, each new JSON built via minimalModelJson helper or an explicit inline literal consistent with existing style. Minor polish opportunity (not a finding): the explicitNull test asserts only model.configValues[...] and not sysConfig.defaultValue — the downstream map value strictly implies the Config emission after preProcess(), so this is equivalent coverage and does not warrant a change. Task 1 tests are ready to ship.
